summary: The main claim is: While I'm not saying we should now sit back and relax, I think recent evidence has significant implications for designing effective strategies to address AI risk.
As the evidence grows, I think regulators will likely adapt, adjusting policy as the difficulty of the problem becomes clearer.
The main claim is: I think there is a rather close parallel with autonomous cars/vehicles.
The main claim is: 2022) beyond attention-only transformers to transformers with both attention and MLP layers.
When used in language models, they perform better than standard MLPs with elementwise activation functions (but appear very slightly below state of the art).
The main claim is: For player 1: If player 2 chooses X, player 1 should choose A (4 1).

top_comment.author: 2box!
top_comment.text: All of your prompts have the same typo, saying that the first number is the payoff for both players. I have to imagine that this is part of what’s confusing to the AI.
top_comment.claim: The comment's point: All of your prompts have the same typo, saying that the first number is the payoff for both players.

summary: The main claim is: This session focuses on issues at the intersection of AI Safety and Artificial Life.

Organized by Simon McGregor (University of Sussex), Rory Greig (DeepMind), Chris Buckley (University of Sussex) ALIFE 2023 (the 2023 conference on Artificial Life) will feature a Special Session on “(In)human Values and Artificial Agency”.

top_comment.author: Charlie Steiner
top_comment.text: Do you have any insider knowledge about this conference? Do you know if it tends to be interesting, have interesting people, be in touch with reality, etc?
top_comment.claim: The comment's point: Do you have any insider knowledge about this conference?

summary: The main claim is: But I think this, properly understood, is not in opposition to the virtue of caution but is incorporated in it.
That is “risk aversion” and can be part of caution, or in an unbalanced form can become an unwise timidity.
The main claim is: More precisely, I expect that the people who justify voting on these grounds are doing the following reasoning: I’m like the people who share my views in my tendency to vote.
Lots of people who share my views vote, enough to make our collective vote worth my while.
The main claim is: I would see an impairment of model function which correlated closely with the intensity of the impairment.

I came up with my own prompts by extracting topic-specific keywords from topic-specific subsets of wikipedia articles, and then taking a text passage leading up to the keyword as the 'prompt' and the keyword as the 'answer'.
The main claim is: What pops out of self-supervised predictive training is noticeably not a classical agent.
GPT-3 is a huge model, trained on huge data, for predicting text.
The main claim is: Announces the first blog-post day from the Refine incubator, sharing participants' early posts after a week of work on the epistemology of alignment research.
The main claim is: FAR provides services to AI safety researchers to accelerate their research agendas.
